examples: take up a matter for consideration
He took up herpetology at the age of fifty

meanings.definition: begin work or acting in a certain capacity, office or job
take up and practice as one's own

examples: She took her seat on the stage
We took our seats in the orchestra
She took up her position behind the tree

meanings.definition: take up a liquid or a gas either by adsorption or by absorption
take out or up with or as if with a scoop
examples: scoop the sugar out of the container
The cloth takes up the liquid
She drew strength from the minister's words

meanings.definition: take up as if with a sponge
return to a previous location or condition
examples: The painting resumed its old condition when we restored it
